threats.title: Unprotected mintYieldFee Allows Anyone to Steal Yield Fees
threats.hypothesis_description: The `mintYieldFee` function in `Vault.sol` lacks access control, allowing any user to mint the accrued yield fees to themselves.
threats.investigation_rationale: The function is marked `external` but has no `onlyOwner` or `msg.sender == _yieldFeeRecipient` checks. This directly exposes the yield fees to theft by any observer.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Missing access control modifier on external state-changing function

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Anyone can pass their own address as `_recipient` and mint shares

analysis_questions: Is there any hidden access control?
Can an attacker mint more than the accrued fees?
validation_requirements: Test calling `mintYieldFee` from an unauthorized account

threats.preliminary_attack_scenario: Attacker monitors the vault for accrued yield fees and calls `mintYieldFee` with their own address to steal the fees.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Loss of all accrued yield fees

threats.test_coverage_gap: Missing test for unauthorized access to `mintYieldFee`

threats.title: Vault Asset Balance Subsidizes New Deposits Allowing Theft of Idle Assets
threats.hypothesis_description: The `_deposit` function mints shares for the full `_assets` amount but only transfers `_assets - _vaultAssets` from the caller, allowing attackers to steal idle assets.
threats.investigation_rationale: If the vault holds underlying assets (e.g., from direct transfers or incomplete liquidation sweeps), a depositor can receive shares without providing the corresponding assets, diluting existing shareholders and stealing the idle assets.

proof_of_signal.pattern: State update (minting) based on parameter rather than actual transferred amount

preconditions: Vault holds a balance of the underlying asset (`_vaultAssets > 0`)

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: `_assetsDeposit = _assets - _vaultAssets` means caller pays less than they receive shares for
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No requirement that caller provides the full `_assets` amount

analysis_questions: How can assets end up in the vault?
Does the liquidation unit mismatch bug contribute to this?
validation_requirements: Send assets directly to vault, then call deposit and verify free shares are minted

threats.preliminary_attack_scenario: Attacker observes idle assets in the vault, calls `deposit` with `_assets` equal to the idle balance, pays 0 tokens, and receives free shares.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Theft of idle assets, dilution of existing shareholders

threats.test_coverage_gap: Missing test for deposits when vault already holds assets

threats.title: Sponsor Function Can Forcefully Revoke Any User's Prize Eligibility
threats.hypothesis_description: The `sponsor` function allows anyone to deposit on behalf of a receiver and forcefully changes the receiver's delegate to the sponsorship address.
threats.investigation_rationale: By calling `sponsor` with a minimal amount (e.g., 1 wei) for a victim, an attacker can overwrite the victim's delegation to the `SPONSORSHIP_ADDRESS`, immediately reducing their delegated balance to 0 and revoking their eligibility to win prizes.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Unauthorized state modification of another user's delegate

preconditions: Victim has a non-zero balance and is eligible for prizes

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: `_twabController.sponsor(_receiver)` is called without checking if `msg.sender` is authorized
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No authorization check for changing another user's delegate

analysis_questions: Can the victim easily revert this?
Does this affect past TWAB or only future TWAB?
validation_requirements: Test calling `sponsor` for another user and verify their delegate changes

threats.preliminary_attack_scenario: Attacker calls `sponsor(1, victimAddress)`, forcing the victim's delegate to the sponsorship address and ruining their chances of winning.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Loss of potential prize winnings for the victim

threats.test_coverage_gap: Missing test for unauthorized sponsorship delegation

threats.title: Canary Tier Claims Bypass Expansion Thresholds
threats.hypothesis_description: Claiming a canary prize sets `_nextNumberOfTiers` to `_numTiers + 1` directly, bypassing the `claimExpansionThreshold` checks.
threats.investigation_rationale: In `_computeNextNumberOfTiers`, `_nextNumberOfTiers` is initialized to `largestTierClaimed + 2`. If the canary tier is claimed, this equals `_numTiers + 1`. The subsequent threshold checks only increase the tier count, so the initial value persists, causing premature tier dilution.
preconditions: A canary prize is claimed (`largestTierClaimed == numberOfTiers - 1`)

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Initial assignment of `_nextNumberOfTiers` satisfies the maximum expansion without meeting threshold criteria
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Threshold checks do not constrain the initial value

analysis_questions: Does this allow an attacker to infinitely expand tiers?
What is the economic cost of claiming a canary prize?
validation_requirements: Test claiming a canary prize when thresholds are not met and verify tiers expand

threats.preliminary_attack_scenario: Attacker claims a single canary prize. The protocol expands the number of tiers in the next draw, diluting prize sizes across more tiers unnecessarily.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Dilution of prize liquidity across too many tiers
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Tiers will eventually contract if not claimed

threats.test_coverage_gap: Missing test for tier expansion when canary claimed but thresholds not met

threats.title: Unit Mismatch in Liquidate Asset Sweep Leaves Assets Vulnerable
threats.hypothesis_description: The `liquidate` function compares `_amountOut` (shares) with `_vaultAssets` (assets), failing to sweep assets when the exchange rate is > 1.
threats.investigation_rationale: If the exchange rate is greater than 1 (1 share > 1 asset), `_amountOut` will be numerically smaller than the equivalent `_vaultAssets`. The condition `_amountOut >= _vaultAssets` fails, leaving assets in the vault where they can be stolen via the `_deposit` subsidization bug.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Comparison of variables with different units

preconditions: Vault holds underlying assets during liquidation

initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Conversion of shares to assets before comparison

analysis_questions: Can this be reliably triggered by a liquidator?
Does this directly enable the free deposit attack?
validation_requirements: Test liquidation with exchange rate > 1 and verify assets are left in the vault

threats.preliminary_attack_scenario: Liquidator calls `liquidate`. Due to unit mismatch, assets remain in the vault. Attacker immediately calls `deposit` to steal the leftover assets.
threats.test_coverage_gap: Missing test for liquidation asset sweep with exchange rate > 1

threats.title: Malicious afterClaimPrize Hook Can DoS Batch Prize Claims
threats.hypothesis_description: A winner can set a reverting `afterClaimPrize` hook, causing any batch claim transaction including them to fail.
threats.investigation_rationale: The `Claimer.claimPrizes` function processes multiple winners in a single transaction. If one winner's hook reverts, the entire batch fails, wasting the claimer's gas and delaying claims for legitimate winners.

proof_of_signal.pattern: External call to untrusted contract without try/catch

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Hook failure propagates and reverts the entire transaction

analysis_questions: Can claimers easily exclude the malicious winner?
Does this affect the VRGDA fee calculation for others?
validation_requirements: Test batch claim with one reverting hook and verify entire batch fails

threats.preliminary_attack_scenario: Attacker wins a prize, sets a reverting hook. Claimer attempts to claim for 100 users including the attacker. Transaction reverts, claimer loses gas.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Gas griefing for claimers, delayed prizes for users
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Claimer must manually exclude the attacker

threats.test_coverage_gap: Missing test for reverting hooks in batch claims

threats.title: Floor Division in Distribution Computation Drains Tier Liquidity
threats.hypothesis_description: Floor division in `_computeNewDistributions` systematically allocates remainder liquidity to the reserve, slowly draining tier allocations.
threats.investigation_rationale: When calculating `deltaPrizeTokensPerShare`, the division is floored. The remainder is explicitly added to the reserve. Over thousands of draws, this systematic rounding down siphons liquidity away from the prize tiers and into the reserve.
preconditions: Large number of total shares causing significant remainders

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Remainder is always added to reserve, never carried forward for tiers

analysis_questions: How much liquidity is lost per draw?
Does this significantly impact prize sizes over a year?
validation_requirements: Simulate 1000 draws and measure the drift between expected tier liquidity and actual

threats.preliminary_attack_scenario: Not a direct attack, but a systemic flaw that reduces user payouts in favor of the protocol reserve over time.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Draw manager can manually redistribute reserve

threats.test_coverage_gap: Missing long-term simulation tests for liquidity drift

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Reserve growing faster than reserveShares dictates

threats.title: Silent Truncation of Large Contributions Causes Loss of Funds
threats.hypothesis_description: Casting `_contributionsForDraw` to `uint96` silently truncates large contributions, permanently locking excess tokens.
threats.investigation_rationale: In `PrizePool.closeDraw`, the total contributions are cast from `uint256` to `uint96`. If contributions exceed `type(uint96).max` (approx 79.2 billion tokens), the value is truncated. The excess tokens remain in the contract but are never distributed.
deployment_assumptions: Prize token has a high total supply and low per-token value (e.g., PEPE, SHIB)
threats.config_evidence: Depends on the ERC20 token used as `prizeToken`
preconditions: Contributions for a single draw exceed `type(uint96).max`

analysis_questions: Is it realistic for a supported token to exceed this limit?
Can the locked tokens be recovered?
validation_requirements: Test `closeDraw` with contributions > `type(uint96).max` and verify truncation

threats.preliminary_attack_scenario: A high-supply token is used. Contributions exceed the limit. The excess is truncated and permanently locked in the PrizePool.
threats.test_coverage_gap: Missing test for massive contribution amounts

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Accounted balance diverges from actual balance

threats.title: Setting Yield Fee to 100% Causes Permanent DoS in Liquidation
threats.hypothesis_description: Setting `_yieldFeePercentage` to `FEE_PRECISION` causes a division by zero in `liquidate`, bricking the vault.
threats.investigation_rationale: The setter allows the fee to equal `FEE_PRECISION`. In `liquidate`, the calculation `(FEE_PRECISION - _yieldFeePercentage)` becomes zero, causing a revert. This permanently halts yield liquidation.
preconditions: Owner sets yield fee to exactly `FEE_PRECISION`

analysis_questions: Can the owner easily fix this by lowering the fee?
Does this affect already accrued fees?
validation_requirements: Test setting fee to 1e9 and calling liquidate

threats.preliminary_attack_scenario: Owner mistakenly sets fee to 100%. All subsequent liquidations fail. Yield cannot be converted to prizes.
threats.test_coverage_gap: Missing test for 100% yield fee edge case

threats.title: Unnecessary Unlimited Asset Approval to Liquidation Pair
threats.hypothesis_description: The vault grants the liquidation pair unlimited approval to spend its underlying asset, exposing assets if the LP is compromised.
threats.investigation_rationale: The liquidation flow only requires the LP to receive vault shares and send prize tokens. It never needs to pull underlying assets from the vault. Granting `type(uint256).max` approval violates the principle of least privilege.
preconditions: Liquidation pair contract is compromised or malicious

analysis_questions: Does the LP ever actually need this approval?
What is the impact if the LP calls `transferFrom`?
validation_requirements: Verify LP implementation does not require asset approval

threats.preliminary_attack_scenario: A malicious LP contract is set by a compromised owner. The LP calls `asset.transferFrom(vault, attacker, balance)` to steal all underlying assets.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Theft of all underlying assets in the vault

threats.test_coverage_gap: Missing security review of LP integration requirements

technical_investigation_focus.execution_timing: Any time after LP is set
